input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this .NET project. They haven't run any coverage yet, so I need to:

1. First explore the project structure to understand what we're working with
2. Set up and run code coverage
3. Analyze the results

Let me start by exploring the codebase structure.

data.content: Now I'll run the tests with code coverage collection enabled. The project already has `coverlet.collector` installed.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-f3dc55b219b941598415aa1c10b3d59d && dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./TestResults --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -30

data.reasoningText: Now I have a clear picture of the codebase. Let me run code coverage using coverlet (which is already referenced in the test project) and then analyze the results. I'll use `dotnet test` with coverage collection enabled.
